steps.description: Enter your phone number or search by name and location
Click 'Click to Remove' on your listing

optout.notes: Navigate to the manage page. Search by phone number or name/location. Click the blue 'Click to Remove' link. Removed data may persist in backups for up to 1 year.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, Incogni. Also check 411.com which is related (owned by WhitePages/Neustar).

scan.reason: Employment background check service, no public consumer search.

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Largest privately held, minority-owned global background screening provider. Background check data subject to FCRA exemptions from CCPA deletion rights. Contact VP of Compliance for data protection requests.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Listed on CFPB consumer reporting companies list. Source: accurate.com privacy policy.

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator. No consumer-facing search. Must request preemptively.

optout.notes: One of the largest data brokers globally. Holds data on ~2.5 billion consumers.

meta.notes: Rebranded from Acxiom to Kinesso but opt-out portal still on acxiom.com domain.

steps.description: Go to the Addresses.com opt-out page
Enter your name as it appears on the listing

optout.notes: Owned by PeopleConnect (parent of Intelius). Opt-out through addresses.com/optout.php or PeopleConnect Suppression Center. Listings typically removed within 48-72 hours. May need to re-submit periodically as data refreshes.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, Incogni. Part of PeopleConnect/Intelius network.

scan.reason: B2B data and identity platform. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to Adstra privacy request form (hosted by OneTrust)
Enter your first and last name
Enter your city, state, and zip code

optout.notes: Uses OneTrust portal for privacy requests. Requires full address information (city, state, zip) in addition to name and email. Formerly known as ALC (American List Counsel).

meta.notes: Source: Optery opt-out guide, California Data Broker Registry. Identity resolution and data enrichment company (formerly ALC/American List Counsel). Provides demographic, psychographic, and purchase data for marketing. Uses OneTrust for privacy request management.

steps.description: Go to the opt-out removal page
Search for your record and click Remove
Check email for confirmation link and click it

optout.notes: Uses publicly available information from public records. Opt-out processed within 24-48 hours after email verification. Records may reappear and require repeated opt-out. CCPA opt-out via 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' link on site.

meta.notes: Public records aggregator categorized as identity verification service. Sacramento, CA based. Source: Privacy Rights Clearinghouse, California Data Broker Registry.

steps.description: Verify email via link from PeopleConnect (15 min expiry)
Select your record and set to Suppressed

optout.notes: Operated by Intelius/PeopleConnect. Opt-out through PeopleConnect Suppression Center covers AnyWho and all other PeopleConnect subsidiaries.

meta.notes: Subsidiary of Intelius/PeopleConnect. Single suppression covers all PeopleConnect brands. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, Incogni

scan.reason: B2B sales intelligence platform. Requires account to search.

steps.description: Check email for verification link from Apollo

optout.notes: Apollo handles deletion and opt-out requests the same way. Profile URL can be included for faster processing. Also available at apollo.io/claim/person/remove. Scrapes LinkedIn extensively.

meta.notes: Source: apollo.io/privacy-policy, apollo.io/company/privacy-center, California Data Broker Registry. B2B sales intelligence with 275M+ contacts. Scrapes LinkedIn, company websites, job boards. CCPA privacy statement last updated August 2025.

steps.description: Select your listing and click opt-out

optout.notes: Web form requires finding specific listing. Email fallback works.

scan.reason: B2B intent data platform. No consumer-facing search. Tracks business content consumption.

steps.description: Click opt-out button to stop online interaction tracking by Bombora and partners

optout.notes: Bombora provides B2B 'intent data' that tracks what topics businesses are researching. Opt-out stops tracking of online interactions. CCPA rights include opt-out of sale and sharing of personal information.

meta.notes: Source: bombora.com/privacy-policy, bombora.com/opt-out. B2B intent data company that tracks business content consumption patterns across a cooperative of 5,000+ B2B websites. Data used by sales and marketing teams to identify companies researching specific topics.

steps.description: Go to CallerSmart and search for your phone number
Scroll to the bottom of the page
Click the 'Do not sell my personal data' link
In the message field, include your phone number, name, address, and profile URL

optout.notes: Uses a contact form for opt-out via 'Do not sell my personal data' link in the footer. Include as much identifying information as possible in the message field. Phone-number-centric search site.

meta.notes: Source: Optery opt-out guides. Phone number lookup and caller ID service with crowd-sourced data.

steps.description: Go to CheckPeople Do Not Sell page (or click link in footer)
Check email for Confirm Request button and click it

optout.notes: Opt-out requests take 5-7 days. Confirmation email may go to spam folder. Can also contact via phone at (800) 267-2122.

meta.notes: Background check and people search service. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

scan.reason: Employment background check service, no public consumer search.

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Background screening data is exempt from CCPA under FCRA. GDPR requests go to dpo@checkr.com. General privacy requests go to hello@checkr.com. Deletion of background check data (criminal history, MVR) is generally not required under CCPA due to FCRA exemption.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated consumer reporting agency. Background check data exempt from CCPA. Source: Checkr privacy policy, CFPB listing.

scan.reason: Consumer reporting agency. No public search. Consumers can request their own disclosure report.

methods.notes: Call 877-OPTOUT5 to opt out of prescreen lists for 5 years

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty consumer reporting agency. Opt-out removes name from prescreen lists for firm offers of credit/insurance for 5 years (consumers 21+). Security freeze available to block release of consumer file without authorization. Disputes of inaccurate information are a separate process. Use caution when emailing sensitive information to chexsystems.cr@fisglobal.com.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty CRA for banking/checking account history. Subsidiary of FIS. FCRA obligations apply. Source: chexsystems.com privacy policy, CFPB consumer reporting companies list.

scan.reason: Consumer reporting agency. No public search. Consumers can request one free report every 12 months.

methods.notes: Call Clarity Consumer Support team to opt out of mailings or request data deletion

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty CRA owned by Experian. Provides subprime consumer information and reports to lenders for thin-file/no-file consumer segments. Opt-out of receiving mailings via phone at 866-390-3118 or by mail to Clarity Services, Inc., PO Box 16, Allen, TX 75013. Data deletion requests subject to FCRA exemptions. One free report available every 12 months. Experian privacy portal can also be used for data deletion requests at 833-210-4615.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty CRA owned by Experian. Focuses on subprime/thin-file consumer segments. Registered on Privacy Rights Clearinghouse and California Data Broker Registry. FCRA obligations apply. Source: clarityservices.com, CFPB listing, Privacy Rights Clearinghouse.

scan.reason: Law enforcement tool. No consumer-facing search. Requires submitting photo to check.

steps.description: Go to Clearview AI privacy request form
Upload a clear photo of your face for matching

optout.notes: Requires uploading a photo of yourself for facial recognition matching. Available to residents of CA, CO, CT, IL, UT, VA. High-priority target due to mass facial recognition surveillance. Clearview scrapes billions of photos from social media and the open web.

meta.notes: Source: clearview.ai/privacy-and-requests, California Data Broker Registry. Clearview scrapes photos from social media including Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter. Multiple lawsuits and fines globally (GDPR fines in UK, Italy, France, Greece). BIPA lawsuit settlement in Illinois. Sells primarily to law enforcement.

steps.description: Enter the URL of your listing

optout.notes: Database of 200 million people and 107 million addresses. Opt-out should be processed immediately. Clear browser cache after submitting to verify removal. Data may reappear as they continuously scrape the internet.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List. Massive database with address and neighbor data. Fast removal but high relisting rate.

scan.reason: B2B contact search platform. Requires account to search.

steps.description: Paste the URL of your listing on ContactOut

optout.notes: May need to find your listing URL in the ContactOut directory first. Removal typically takes 5-7 days. Data commonly reappears as ContactOut continuously scrapes public records and LinkedIn. Scrapes LinkedIn profiles.

meta.notes: Source: contactout.com, Optery opt-out guide. Scrapes email addresses and phone numbers from LinkedIn profiles of 300M+ professionals at 30M+ companies. High relisting rate due to continuous scraping.

scan.reason: B2B real estate and property data aggregator. No consumer-facing search. Claims CCPA exemptions for most consumer data.

optout.notes: Rebranded to Cotality in 2025. Claims CCPA exemptions for most consumer data (only HR and B2B data subjects can exercise CCPA rights). Opt-out triggers 5-year suppression period. May deny requests from non-employees/non-B2B contacts.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, CoreLogic CCPA page. Rebranded to Cotality 2025. Claims broad CCPA exemptions — opt-out may be limited for general consumers.

optout.notes: Non-profit operated by Free Law Project. Will generally block search engines from indexing specific pages upon written request (de-indexing via robots meta tag or x-robots-tag HTTP header). Will NOT remove information from the site itself unless presented with a court sealing/removal order. CCPA does not apply to non-profits or public records. Legal matters can be sent to legal@free.law.

meta.notes: Non-profit free legal search engine by Free Law Project. Open source (github.com/freelawproject/courtlistener). CCPA does not apply. De-indexing from search engines is the primary remedy. Source: CourtListener terms of service, Free Law Project contact page.

scan.reason: Ad-tech platform. No consumer-facing search. Tracks users via cookies and device IDs.

steps.description: Go to Criteo Privacy Rights page
Click the 'Disable Criteo Services' button to opt out of personalized ads
For data deletion, go to Criteo Privacy page
Use the contact form for GDPR right to be forgotten

optout.notes: Criteo is a major retargeting ad platform. 'Disable Criteo Services' button immediately stops personalized ads. For full data deletion, email dpo@criteo.com. GDPR adopted as worldwide standard. Criteo will provide an Excel spreadsheet of all personal data upon request.

meta.notes: Source: criteo.com/privacy, criteo.com/privacy/ccpa-privacy-policy. Major ad-tech retargeting platform (Paris-based, publicly traded). Tracks users across websites via cookies and device fingerprinting. GDPR and CCPA compliant. DPO contact: dpo@criteo.com.

scan.reason: Personality prediction platform. Requires account to search profiles.

optout.notes: Email hello@crystalknows.com to request deletion of account and all associated data. Crystal deletes personal information and private data, though some latency may exist for backup storage. GDPR, CCPA, and SOC2 compliant. Analyzes writing style and public LinkedIn profiles to predict DISC personality types.

meta.notes: Source: crystalknows.com/privacy, crystalknows.com/gdpr. Personality AI platform that predicts DISC personality types from LinkedIn profiles and writing samples. Does not store sensitive PII externally. Scrapes LinkedIn for profile data and analyzes writing style.

optout.notes: CCPA removal page at /ccparemoval. Typically removes within 12-24 hours but may take up to 3 days. Profile can return as new records appear.

meta.notes: Also has CCPA-specific removal at /ccparemoval. Source: State of Surveillance Master List, IntelTechniques Workbook

scan.reason: B2B marketing data provider. No consumer-facing search. Provides targeted mailing and email lists to businesses.

optout.notes: Formerly known as Infogroup, InfoUSA, and Data.com — rebranded to Data Axle. Has email addresses for 200M people, homeowner info for 110M+, household data for 165M+. Opt-out suppresses data (marked 'do not share') but retains it. Collects from 100+ sources including tax assessments, voter registration, utility connections.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registered as Data Axle, Inc. f/k/a Infogroup Inc.), Privacy Rights Clearinghouse. One of the largest marketing list providers in the US.

scan.reason: B2B marketing list provider. No consumer-facing search. Sells targeted business and consumer mailing lists.

optout.notes: Opt-out via 'Your California Privacy Choices' link in website footer (works for all states). Requires email confirmation. Claims 22M businesses, 43M business executives, 270M consumers. Processing takes 15-45 days.

meta.notes: Source: DatabaseUSA privacy policy, JoinDeleteMe opt-out guide. Based in Omaha, NE. Claims 98% coverage of Americans in consumer lists.

scan.reason: B2B direct mail marketing services provider. No consumer-facing search. Licensed data reseller — does not compile or own data assets.

optout.notes: National Do Not Mail List form at directmail.com/mail_preference/. Must fill out form and confirm via email. Removal is NOT immediate — can take weeks or months as they contact each mailer. DirectMail.com is a licensed data reseller, not a data compiler. Privacy policy is extremely brief and lacks consumer rights details.

meta.notes: Source: DirectMail.com privacy policy, JoinDeleteMe opt-out guide. 50+ year old company. Based in Prince Frederick, MD. Privacy policy acknowledged to be minimal.

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator focused on business data. No consumer-facing search. Consumer data collected for business contact intelligence.

optout.notes: Privacy Choices form requires email verification after submission. D&B should respond within 15 days. CCPA opt-out also available by calling 1-855-549-8900.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, D&B privacy portal. Primarily B2B business data but holds personal data on business contacts and executives.

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator and marketing platform. No consumer-facing search. Must submit preemptive opt-out.

methods.postal_address: Epsilon, PO Box 1478, Broomfield, CO 80038, Attn: Privacy

optout.notes: Holds data on 200+ million US consumers. DSR portal supports opt-out of sale, sharing, targeted advertising, and profiling. Lotame merged into Epsilon Oct 2025.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Epsilon privacy portal. Lotame Solutions merged into Epsilon Data Management LLC effective Oct 2025.

scan.reason: B2B credit reporting and data aggregator. Consumer credit reports handled via separate Equifax consumer portal. Marketing data opt-out handled separately.

optout.notes: Multiple opt-out paths needed. OptOutPrescreen.com handles pre-approved credit/insurance offers (shared with Experian, TransUnion, Innovis). Online opt-out lasts 5 years; permanent opt-out requires postal mail. Equifax myPrivacy portal for marketing data.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Equifax privacy portal. Major credit bureau — data broker under Texas law. 2017 breach exposed 147M records.

scan.reason: B2B marketing list provider. No consumer-facing search. Sells targeted email and mailing lists to businesses.

optout.notes: Exact Data is owned by Data Axle. Privacy requests directed to Data Axle privacy team. May retain data even after opt-out for compliance, fraud prevention, and dispute resolution purposes. Identity verification including government ID may be required.

meta.notes: Source: Exact Data privacy policy. Owned by Data Axle — privacy requests go through Data Axle privacy team at privacyteam@data-axle.com.

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator and marketing platform. Consumer credit reports handled separately via Experian consumer portal.

optout.notes: Opt-out lasts 5 years, then must re-submit. Separate opt-outs for direct mail, telemarketing, and targeted online advertising. Also use OptOutPrescreen.com for pre-approved credit offers.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Experian privacy portal. Experian Marketing Solutions LLC is a registered data broker under Texas law. Opt-out only lasts 5 years.

steps.description: Enter first, middle, and last name
Click Opt Out button on your record

optout.notes: CAPTCHA required. Opt-out removes from all places on the site. Does not remove from original public record sources.

meta.notes: Genealogy-focused people search. Phone support at 877-838-4889. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, IntelTechniques Workbook

steps.description: Agree that you are the subject of the record
Click 'Click here to remove' button in verification email

optout.notes: Straightforward removal. Email subject line should be 'Request for Deletion of Personal Data'. Can also call (866) 679-8725 or mail PO BOX 55071, Boston, MA 02205-5071.

meta.notes: Updates automatically so data may reappear. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

scan.reason: Employment background check service, no public consumer search.

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. CCPA and other data privacy laws do not apply to First Advantage lines of business governed by FCRA (employment background screening, resident screening). Marketing opt-out via privacy@fadv.com or employment.solutions@fadv.com. In some instances opting out may require employer authorization.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Global background screening provider. Acquired Sterling. Source: fadv.com privacy center.

scan.reason: B2B location intelligence platform. Consumer data collected via apps (Foursquare City Guide, Swarm) and SDK partners.

steps.description: Go to Foursquare data requests page
Select opt-out of sale / data deletion
Go to Foursquare Privacy Portal (via Atlassian)

optout.notes: Foursquare acquired Factual in 2020, consolidating two major location data companies. Opt-out of sale, sharing, targeted advertising, and sensitive data use all available. May share data with law enforcement in response to court orders. Claims to never sell GPS data to government entities.

meta.notes: Source: foursquare.com/legal/privacy-center, California Data Broker Registry (registered as Foursquare Labs, Inc.). Merged with Factual in 2020. Major location intelligence provider. Collects location data from own apps and via SDK embedded in third-party apps. May disclose data to law enforcement via court orders but claims not to sell GPS data to government.

scan.reason: B2B identity resolution API. No consumer-facing search.

optout.notes: FullContact marks data as 'hidden' and restricts processing rather than fully deleting. Profile removed from identity graph. Scrapes social media profiles including LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook.

meta.notes: Source: fullcontact.com/privacy/privacy-policy, California Data Broker Registry. Identity resolution platform that builds profiles by linking social media accounts, email addresses, and public data. Scrapes LinkedIn and other social networks.

scan.reason: Employment background check service, no public consumer search.

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Personal information collected for background checks is largely exempt from CCPA under FCRA and GLBA exemptions. Privacy requests should be sent to privacy@goodhire.com.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated background check provider. Operates as service provider to employers. Source: GoodHire privacy policy.

scan.reason: B2B location intelligence platform. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to Gravy Analytics Opt-Out/Do Not Sell page

optout.notes: Requires mobile Advertising ID for opt-out. Suffered a major data breach in January 2025 exposing location data of millions of users. Sold location data to law enforcement and government agencies via contractors.

meta.notes: Source: gravyanalytics.com/privacy-policy, gravyanalytics.com/opt-out-do-not-sell. Location analytics company. Major data breach in January 2025 exposed precise location data. Known to sell data to government contractors and law enforcement agencies. Parent company Unacast acquired Gravy Analytics.

scan.reason: Employment background check service, no public consumer search.

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. CCPA does not apply to background screening data. HireRight abides by opt-out provisions of CCPA for non-screening data. Privacy requests go to DPO at dpo@hireright.com. Has GDPR obligations for EU/UK data subjects.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Listed on CFPB consumer reporting companies list. Source: HireRight legal pages, datarequests.org.

scan.notes: Hiya does not offer a public web search. Caller ID data is accessible through the Hiya app and integrated Samsung phone features.

methods.description: Open Hiya app > Settings > Privacy > Deactivate Account > Confirm

steps.description: Open the Hiya app on your smartphone

optout.notes: Deactivating your account does not guarantee data deletion from their database. Hiya sells caller profile information to telecom companies. Contact support@hiya.com to explicitly request data deletion under CCPA. Hiya is pre-installed on many Samsung phones.

meta.notes: Source: DoNotPay, Hiya Help Center. B2B caller ID provider integrated into Samsung and carrier apps. Account deactivation may not fully remove data.

scan.reason: Email discovery API. Requires account to search. Can check via hunter.io/email-verifier.

steps.description: Check email for verification from Hunter

steps.value: Delete the data associated with this email address

optout.notes: Claim page gives full control over your data. Can update or entirely remove indexed data. Scrapes company websites and public sources for email patterns.

meta.notes: Source: hunter.io/privacy-policy, hunter.io/gdpr. Email discovery and verification tool. Indexes professional email addresses from publicly accessible web pages. Does not scrape LinkedIn directly but collects email patterns from company websites.

steps.description: Click Confirm Email button in email from InfoTracer

methods.notes: Privacy experts available 8am-6pm EST, 7 days a week

optout.notes: Typically removed within 24-48 hours. InfoTracer only uses submitted info for removal purpose. Compliance Team at privacy@infotracer.com.

meta.notes: Fast removal (24-48 hours). Phone support available 7 days/week. Source: State of Surveillance Master List, IntelTechniques Workbook

scan.reason: Consumer reporting agency. No public search. Consumers can request their own credit report.

steps.description: Go to OptOutPrescreen.com (joint initiative with all 4 major CRAs)
Submit opt-out request to all four bureaus

methods.notes: Call to opt out of marketing lists

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated consumer reporting agency (the 4th major credit bureau). Opt-out via OptOutPrescreen.com covers all 4 bureaus. Security freeze available with 10-digit PIN. Privacy Officer reachable at privacy@innovis.com or 1-855-871-3076. EU/UK/Swiss and some US state residents can request data erasure.

meta.notes: 4th major US credit bureau. FCRA-regulated CRA. FCRA obligations apply. Source: innovis.com, OptOutPrescreen.com.

steps.description: Verify email via link sent by PeopleConnect (15 min expiry)
Select your record and set to Suppressed

optout.notes: Part of PeopleConnect network. Suppression via PeopleConnect covers Intelius, TruthFinder, US Search, ZabaSearch, AnyWho, Addresses.com, and more.

meta.notes: Owned by PeopleConnect. Single suppression covers all PeopleConnect brands. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

steps.description: Verify email via link sent by PeopleConnect (15 min expiry)
Select your record and set to Suppressed

optout.notes: Owned by PeopleConnect. Opting out also suppresses records on TruthFinder, Instant Checkmate, US Search, ZabaSearch, AnyWho, Addresses.com, and other PeopleConnect subsidiaries.

meta.notes: Parent company PeopleConnect owns many subsidiary people-search brands. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, IntelTechniques Workbook

scan.reason: Search function has been intermittently disabled. Site aggregates hundreds of millions of court records nationwide.

optout.notes: No dedicated opt-out page or privacy email found. The site temporarily disabled its search function after reports of publishing confidential ethics and juvenile records. Contact through the site's terms page or general contact methods. Public records are typically exempt from CCPA. Court records aggregator with limited consumer-facing removal options.

meta.notes: Free nationwide court case search engine. Has published confidential records including juvenile court records. Limited opt-out options. Source: ABA Journal reporting, judyrecords.com terms page.

scan.reason: B2B contact finder via browser extension. Requires account to search.

methods.notes: Send email with subject line 'Opt-out Kaspr'

steps.description: Go to Kaspr privacy portal (hosted by Mine)
Enter your contact details and email

optout.notes: Fined 240,000 EUR by CNIL (France) for GDPR violations related to scraping contact details of people who limited LinkedIn visibility. Ordered to stop collecting data from people who chose to limit profile visibility. Scrapes LinkedIn profiles.

meta.notes: Source: kaspr.io/privacy-policy, help.kaspr.io, CNIL enforcement action. LinkedIn scraper fined by French DPA (CNIL) for scraping data from people who restricted their LinkedIn profile visibility. Owned by Cognism. Uses 'legitimate interest' as lawful basis under GDPR.

scan.reason: B2B mobile attribution and data platform. No consumer-facing search. Sells geolocation data and mobile advertising IDs.

optout.notes: FTC settlement approved Nov 2025 — Kochava must implement consumer opt-out web form, privacy block for sensitive venues (healthcare, schools, jails), obtain proof of consumer consent from data suppliers, and 2-year injunction. Opt-out requests are device-specific — submit each Mobile Advertising ID separately.

meta.notes: Source: FTC enforcement action, Kochava privacy policy. FTC settlement approved Nov 2025 requires privacy block feature for sensitive venues and consumer opt-out mechanism.

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator. No public consumer search.

methods.postal_address: LexisNexis Consumer Center, P.O. Box 105108, Atlanta, GA 30348-5108

optout.notes: One of the largest data aggregators. May require ID verification. Very slow processing.

meta.notes: Extremely large data holdings. Subsidiaries include Accurint. Slow but generally compliant.

scan.reason: B2B data collaboration platform. No consumer-facing search. Provides identity resolution and audience data to advertisers.

optout.notes: LiveRamp stores opt-out in a database and checks all future uploads against it. Opt-out completed within 15 business days. Deletion requests within 45 days. Available to all US residents regardless of state.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, LiveRamp privacy portal. Major data collaboration platform — enables identity resolution across devices and channels. DAA member.

steps.description: Fill in the opt-out form with your data
Check email for verification link and click it to complete the request

optout.notes: Fill out the opt-out form and confirm via email verification link. Features contact details, location history, and education records for US residents.

meta.notes: Source: Incogni opt-out guides, Optery. People-search directory focused on US residents.

scan.reason: B2B audience management and data management platform. No consumer-facing search. Merged into Epsilon Oct 2025.

optout.notes: Lotame merged into Epsilon Data Management LLC effective Oct 1, 2025. Epsilon now provides Lotame products under the Lotame name. Privacy Manager processes opt-outs within hours; Panorama ID may take up to 7 days to expire.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry. Lotame Solutions Inc merged into Epsilon Data Management LLC effective Oct 1, 2025. Lotame brand continues under Epsilon.

scan.reason: B2B sales intelligence platform. Requires account to search.

steps.description: Go to Lusha removal request page
Check email for confirmation link and click it

methods.notes: CCPA toll-free line. Use service code 2001#

optout.notes: Automated removal form permanently deletes data. Lusha maintains a suppression list to prevent re-collection. Also has a 'Do Not Sell' link on homepage. Scrapes LinkedIn profiles extensively.

meta.notes: Source: lusha.com/privacy-center/request-removal, California Data Broker Registry. B2B contact data platform. GDPR, CCPA, SOC 2, and ISO27701 certified. Primary data source is LinkedIn profile scraping via browser extension. Suppression list prevents re-collection after opt-out.

scan.reason: B2B data quality and marketing list provider. No consumer-facing search. Provides address verification, data enrichment, and mailing lists.

optout.notes: Two email paths: optout@melissa.com for general opt-out and CCPArequest@melissa.com for CCPA-specific requests. Include full name (with variations), mailing address, phone, and email. 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link in website footer.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registered as Melissa Corporation), Melissa privacy policy, Optery opt-out guide. 20+ years in data solutions.

steps.description: Go to MyLife CCPA opt-out page

optout.notes: Include profile link in email requests. If form does not work, email privacy@mylife.com or customercare@mylife.com. Removal usually takes 7-14 business days, up to 30 days.

meta.notes: Assigns reputation scores to individuals. Difficult opt-out process. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

scan.reason: Consumer reporting agency for telecom/utility data. No public search. Consumers can request one free disclosure report every 12 months.

methods.notes: Call to opt out of pre-approved credit offers using NCTUE data

steps.description: Go to NCTUE consumer portal for security freeze

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty consumer reporting agency for telecom, pay TV, security, internet, and utility payment data. Member-exclusive data exchange. Security freeze available via consumer portal (nctueconsumerportal.com) or by calling 866-349-5355. Opt-out of pre-approved credit offers at 888-327-4376 or by mail to Exchange Service Center - NCTUE / Opt Out, PO Box 105398, Atlanta, GA 30348-5398. Disputes via mail to PO Box 105398 or call 866-343-2821. No specific privacy email found.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty CRA for telecom/utility payment data. FCRA obligations apply. Managed by Equifax. Source: nctue.com, CFPB consumer reporting companies list, Privacy Rights Clearinghouse.

steps.description: Search for your name on Neighbor Report
Copy the URL of your specific listing
Enter the URL of your listing
Check email for confirmation link and click it

optout.notes: Address-focused people search site that also shows neighbors. Requires email verification. Find your listing first, copy the URL, then submit removal request.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List. Address and neighbor-focused people search.

scan.reason: B2B audience measurement and marketing data platform. No consumer-facing search.

optout.notes: Nielsen Marketing Cloud opt-out removes all information associated with email. NielsenIQ (NIQ) is a separate entity — contact connect.privacy@nielseniq.com for NIQ data.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registered as Nielsen Marketing Cloud), Nielsen privacy portal. Nielsen and NielsenIQ are now separate companies.

steps.description: Scroll to the bottom of the page
Click 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' link
You will be redirected to the BeenVerified opt-out page
Enter your first name and last name
Select your state from the drop-down
Find and select your listing from the results
Check inbox for confirmation email from BeenVerified and click 'Verify Opt-Out'

optout.notes: Owned by BeenVerified. Opt-out redirects to BeenVerified's suppression center. If you have already opted out of BeenVerified, your NumberGuru data should also be suppressed. Phone-number-focused people search.

meta.notes: Source: Optery, OneRep. Owned by BeenVerified. Opt-out flows through BeenVerified suppression system.

steps.description: Paste the URL of your Nuwber listing
Click CONFIRM REQUEST button in verification email

optout.notes: Each listing URL must be removed individually if multiple exist. Email support@nuwber.com if data reappears after opt-out.

meta.notes: Requires finding listing URL first. Multiple listings must each be removed separately. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator. No consumer-facing search. Oracle shut down Advertising services Sept 2024 but Data Cloud opt-out portal remains.

optout.notes: Oracle shut down Oracle Advertising services in Sept 2024 but the Data Cloud opt-out portal at datacloudoptout.oracle.com remains operational. Acquired BlueKai and Datalogix.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Oracle privacy portal. Oracle Advertising decommissioned Sept 2024 but Data Cloud opt-out still active.

steps.description: Search for your name on PeekYou
Copy your unique profile ID from the URL
Go to PeekYou opt-out page (link in footer: Do Not Sell My Personal Information)
Enter your profile URL or unique ID
Check email for verification link from PeekYou

optout.notes: Find your profile via search, then use the Do Not Sell link in the footer. Requires CAPTCHA and email verification. Information may reappear if databases are refreshed.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, DataBrokersWatch.org. Aggregates social media profiles and web presence data.

steps.description: Click Next under Public Record Removal
Check email for confirmation link and click it

optout.notes: CAPTCHA required. Email verification needed. May take 3-9 days to process.

meta.notes: Major people-search site. Data may reappear after 6-12 months. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

steps.description: Go to PeopleLooker opt-out search page
Select your record and click opt-out
Check email from BeenVerified and click Verify Opt-Out link

optout.notes: Owned by BeenVerified. Verification email comes from BeenVerified. Contact privacy@peoplelooker.com if new records appear after opt-out.

meta.notes: Subsidiary of BeenVerified. New records can reappear from data partners. Source: State of Surveillance Master List

scan.reason: Requires paid subscription for full results. Facial recognition reverse image search.

steps.description: Go to PimEyes opt-out request form
Upload an anonymized ID scan for verification

optout.notes: Requires uploading a photo and anonymized ID scan. Data retained up to 96 hours after opt-out for processing, then permanently deleted. GDPR complaints filed by Big Brother Watch (UK).

meta.notes: Source: pimeyes.com/en/opt-out-request-form, pimeyes.com/en/privacy-policy. Facial recognition search engine that indexes publicly available photos from the open web. Based in Poland (Belize-registered). Subject to GDPR enforcement actions.

scan.notes: Pipl has changed its business model to B2B only. No public search interface.

optout.notes: B2B identity resolution service. May request identity verification (utility bill or redacted ID). Opt-out places a suppression flag rather than deleting data. Response expected within 14-30 business days. Alternative phone: 1-(415)-373-0180.

meta.notes: Now primarily B2B identity resolution. Suppression flag does not delete data, just hides from results. May require ID verification. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, IntelTechniques Workbook

steps.description: Go to Radaris privacy control page

scan.reason: B2B contact search. Requires account for full results.

steps.description: Click 'Do Not Sell My Info' in footer or use Claim Profile
Enter the company you currently work for
Enter a valid professional or personal email
Check email for verification link from RocketReach
Select Remove My Profile option and confirm

optout.notes: Requires email verification. Profile URL or LinkedIn URL can be included. After verification, choose 'Remove My Profile' from options (Update, Download Data, Remove). Scrapes LinkedIn profiles.

meta.notes: Source: rocketreach.co/privacy, rocketreach.co/ccpa_general, Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List. B2B contact database with 700M+ profiles. Scrapes LinkedIn, company websites, and public data sources.

scan.reason: B2B location data platform. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to SafeGraph Do Not Sell page

optout.notes: Requires your mobile Advertising ID (MAID) for opt-out. On Android: Settings > Google > Ads > Advertising ID. On iOS: Settings > Privacy > Tracking. Sold location data that was used to track visits to abortion clinics, sparking controversy.

meta.notes: Source: safegraph.com/ccpa-privacy-policy, California Data Broker Registry. Aggregates anonymized location data from mobile apps via SDK partners. Controversy over data being used to track visits to sensitive locations (Planned Parenthood, places of worship). Claims not to sell individual-level data but provides foot traffic analytics.

scan.reason: Consumer reporting agency. No public search. Consumers can request their own consumer report.

steps.description: Go to SageStream online opt-out form

methods.notes: Call Monday-Friday 9AM-5PM PT to request opt-out

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty consumer reporting agency, subsidiary of LexisNexis Risk Solutions. Opt-out removes name from prescreen lists for unsolicited credit/insurance offers. Processed within 5 working days, though full removal from client databases may take up to 45 days. Security freeze available via LexisNexis portal. Even after opt-out, data may be retained for legal compliance.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty CRA. Subsidiary of LexisNexis Risk Solutions. Registered on Privacy Rights Clearinghouse. FCRA obligations apply. Source: sagestreamllc.com, Privacy Rights Clearinghouse, CFPB listing.

steps.description: Check email from SearchPeopleFree and click the 'Validate My Email' button
On the redirected search page, enter your name and location, then click Search and select your listing for removal

optout.notes: Multi-step process: submit request, verify email, then search and select your listing on the redirected page. Also check searchpeoplefree.net which may have separate listings. Over 3 million monthly visitors.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, Incogni. High-traffic people search with .com and .net variants.

scan.reason: Ad-tech and data platform. No consumer-facing search. Tracks via sharing widgets.

steps.description: Go to ShareThis Do Not Sell My Data page
Click opt-out button to stop profiling and targeted advertising
Go to ShareThis data subject access request page
Submit data access or deletion request

optout.notes: ShareThis embeds social sharing widgets on millions of websites, collecting browsing data. Do Not Sell page immediately stops profiling and targeted advertising for your browser/device. Separate form for data subject access requests (CCPA/GDPR).

meta.notes: Source: sharethis.com/privacy, California Data Broker Registry. Provides social sharing buttons embedded on millions of websites. Collects browsing behavior data across sites where widgets are installed. Registered as data broker in California.

scan.reason: B2B contact search tool. Requires account to search.

steps.description: Go to SignalHire Do Not Sell page (CA/VA residents)
Submit the Do Not Sell request

optout.notes: Separate forms for opt-out and Do Not Sell (CA/VA). After opt-out, SignalHire retains email on suppression list only. All other data deleted or anonymized. Scrapes LinkedIn profiles.

meta.notes: Source: signalhire.com/privacy, signalhire.com/opt-out, signalhire.com/do-not-sell. B2B contact finder. Scrapes LinkedIn and other professional networks via browser extension. CCPA and GDPR compliant.

steps.description: Click Remove Record next to your listing
Click confirmation link in verification email

optout.notes: Claims 48-hour turnaround but may take up to a week. Use a disposable email for opt-out. Data may be re-added from public sources.

meta.notes: Background check and people search. Data re-acquisition from public sources is common. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List

scan.reason: B2B email finder and outreach platform. Requires account to search.

steps.value: Do Not Sell My Personal Information
steps.description: Click 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' link in footer
Submit the Do Not Sell request

optout.notes: Uses a captcha on the opt-out form. DPO appointed through Privacity GmbH (EU/EEA representative). Collects email patterns from company websites and LinkedIn.

meta.notes: Source: snov.io/privacy-policy, snov.io/knowledgebase/gdpr. Email finder and sales automation platform. Scrapes email addresses from LinkedIn and company websites. Ukraine-based company. GDPR and CCPA compliant.

steps.description: Go to Social Catfish opt-out page
Check email for confirmation from Social Catfish

optout.notes: State selection required. Confirmation emails sent after submission. Social Catfish will delete all verifiable related information within 15 days and take steps to prevent reacquisition.

meta.notes: Source: socialcatfish.com/opt-out, Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List. People search and reverse lookup service that aggregates social media profiles, public records, and online data.

steps.description: Go to the Spokeo opt-out page
Paste the URL of your Spokeo listing
Check email for confirmation link and click it

optout.notes: Requires finding your specific listing URL first. Email confirmation required.

meta.notes: One of the largest people-search sites. High re-listing rate.

scan.reason: Employment background check service, no public consumer search.

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA, now a First Advantage company. Background screening services are not subject to CCPA but are governed by FCRA. Sterling does not sell personal information. Data retained 7 years in active accounts, 3 years after last login for inactive accounts. Fingerprint data deleted after 29 days.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated CRA. Now a First Advantage company. Source: Sterling privacy policy, privacy.sterlingcheck.com.

scan.reason: Consumer reporting agency. No public search. Consumers can request one free report every 12 months.

methods.notes: Call to have name and address removed from prescreen lists for 5 years

optout.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty consumer reporting agency. Opt-out removes name from prescreen lists for unsolicited credit/insurance offers for 5 years. For permanent opt-out, download form from website and mail to: Teletrack Opt-Out Request, PO Box 740008, Atlanta, GA 30374. Disputes of inaccurate information handled separately under FCRA. No specific privacy email found. Free report available every 12 months and must be provided within 15 days of request.

meta.notes: FCRA-regulated specialty CRA focused on non-prime lending. Previously subject to FTC enforcement action. FCRA obligations apply. Source: consumers.teletrack.com, CFPB consumer reporting companies list.

steps.description: Enter street address, city, state, ZIP

optout.notes: Free removal. Include full name, email, phone, and mailing address in email opt-out. 5-7 business day processing.

meta.notes: Continuously pulls from public records so data may reappear. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

scan.reason: B2B programmatic advertising platform. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to The Trade Desk Data Access Portal
Submit data access or deletion request
Go to DAA opt-out page to opt out of targeted ads
Find and opt out of The Trade Desk

optout.notes: The Trade Desk operates UID2 (Unified ID 2.0), an identity framework for advertising. Opt-out via adsrvr.org portal or DAA industry opt-out at aboutads.info/choices. Also supports NAI opt-out at networkadvertising.org/choices.

meta.notes: Source: thetradedesk.com, adsrvr.org, California Data Broker Registry. Largest independent demand-side platform (DSP) for programmatic advertising. Operates UID2 identity framework. Publicly traded (NASDAQ: TTD). Processes billions of ad impressions daily.

scan.reason: B2B investigative intelligence platform. No consumer-facing search. Used by 3,400+ law enforcement agencies.

optout.notes: Subject of $27.5M privacy class action settlement (2025) over secret collection of 40M Californians' data. Settlement requires TR to no longer require driver's license for CA residents to remove data. Email verification required after form submission.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Thomson Reuters privacy portal. $27.5M class action settlement approved 2025. CLEAR aggregates data from thousands of sources for investigative intelligence.

scan.reason: B2B email intelligence and identity resolution platform. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to TowerData CCPA request form

optout.notes: Formerly known as TowerData, now operates as AtData. Email-based identity resolution and data enrichment. Links email addresses to demographic data including age, income, political affiliation, and purchase history.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registered as TowerData, Inc.), towerdata.com/ccpa-form, Optery opt-out guide. Email intelligence platform (formerly Rapleaf). Links email addresses to demographic profiles. Now operates as AtData. Physical address: 33 Irving Place, 3rd Floor, New York, NY 10003.

scan.reason: B2B data aggregator. Consumer credit reports handled separately. TLO and Neustar are B2B investigative and marketing platforms.

optout.notes: TransUnion acquired Neustar and TLO. Separate opt-outs may be needed for each subsidiary. Neustar privacy email: privacy@team.neustar. Use OptOutPrescreen.com for pre-approved credit offers.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, TransUnion/Neustar privacy portals. Multiple subsidiaries (TLO, Neustar) may require separate opt-out requests.

steps.description: Go to your specific listing page
Click the Remove This Record link

meta.notes: Fast removal but very high re-listing rate. CAPTCHA required for web form.

scan.notes: Truecaller does not offer a public web search. Data is accessible via the Truecaller app only.

steps.description: Enter your phone number with country code
Specify your reason for opting out
Copy the SMS code that appears and send it from the phone number you want to unlist

methods.description: If you are a Truecaller user, deactivate your account first via app Settings > Privacy > Deactivate Account before unlisting

optout.notes: Must deactivate account FIRST if you are a Truecaller user, then unlist. The app uploads entire contact lists from users, so your number may be in their database even if you never signed up. Removal takes up to 24 hours. Number may be re-added if a contact saves you and uses the app.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, MakeUseOf. Swedish company with 400M+ users. Collects contact data from users' address books.

steps.description: Verify your email via PeopleConnect to complete opt-out

optout.notes: Part of PeopleConnect network. Opt-out processed within 48 hours. Requires email verification via PeopleConnect. Can also exercise CCPA rights by emailing help@truthfinder.com with applicable law, screenshot of record, full name, DOB, city/state, phone, and email.

meta.notes: People-search/background check hybrid. Part of PeopleConnect. Registered California data broker. High relisting rate. Source: California Data Broker Registry, TruthFinder privacy policy.

scan.reason: B2B marketing data and list provider. No consumer-facing search. Provides targeted marketing lists for businesses.

methods.postal_address: US Data Corporation, 31330 Oak Crest Drive, Suite 102, Westlake Village, CA 91301

optout.notes: Online form options: Do Not Sell, Delete My Information, or Data Access request. Claims 98% of Americans and 17M+ business records. Based in Westlake Village, CA.

meta.notes: Source: US Data Corporation privacy page, JoinDeleteMe opt-out guide, PrivacyHawk. Marketing data agency offering highly targeted data marketing lists.

steps.description: Verify email via link from PeopleConnect (15 min expiry)
Select your record and set to Suppressed

optout.notes: Part of PeopleConnect network. Suppression via PeopleConnect covers all subsidiary sites. May repopulate listing over time; check back in 30-60 days.

meta.notes: Owned by PeopleConnect/Intelius. Information should be removed within 15-30 days. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, State of Surveillance Master List

steps.description: Go to USPhoneBook opt-out page (or click 'do not sell my personal information' in footer)
Enter your phone number and click Search
Check email and click the 'click here to remove [your name]' button

optout.notes: Search by phone number to find your listing. Requires reCAPTCHA and email verification. Removal within 72 hours. Data may reappear as databases are refreshed.

meta.notes: Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List. Phone-number-centric people search. Also check unitedstatesphonebook.com.

scan.reason: B2B location analytics platform. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to Unacast Opt Out & Delete Request page

optout.notes: Median response time is 11 days, mean is 8 days. Also accepts written requests mailed to Unacast, Inc., 245 Fifth Avenue, Suite 1101, New York, NY 10016. Acquired Gravy Analytics. Parent company for Gravy Analytics location data.

meta.notes: Source: unacast.com/opt-out, unacast.com/privacy-policy, California Data Broker Registry. Location analytics company that acquired Gravy Analytics. Subject to California AG investigative sweep of location data industry. Publishes privacy request statistics (median 11 day response).

steps.description: Visit UniCourt Public Records Policy page for redaction request
Provide reason for request (privacy concerns, sealed records, etc.)

optout.notes: Public records are typically exempt from CCPA. UniCourt will only fully redact/delete records sealed or expunged by court order. For unsealed records, UniCourt may de-index URLs from search engines but records remain on platform. Only accepts requests from parties to the record, legal guardians, attorneys, or fiduciaries. Does not accept requests from reputation companies.

meta.notes: Court records aggregator. Public records largely exempt from privacy laws. Full removal requires court sealing/expungement order. Source: UniCourt privacy policy, public records policy.

scan.reason: B2B location data provider. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to Veraset Do Not Sell My Info page
Submit the Do Not Sell request

optout.notes: Requires mobile Advertising ID for opt-out. Veraset provides raw GPS data to academic researchers, hedge funds, and government agencies.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry (registered as Veraset). Provides raw GPS location data derived from mobile devices. Clients include academic researchers, hedge funds, real estate firms, and government agencies. Data collected via SDK partners embedded in mobile apps.

scan.reason: B2B data analytics and marketing solutions provider. No consumer-facing search. Consumer opt-out via Infutor privacy portal.

optout.notes: Verisk acquired Infutor Data Solutions. Consumer opt-out portal at privacy.infutor.com. Identity verification may be required. Removal within 45 days per CCPA.

meta.notes: Source: California Data Broker Registry, Verisk/Infutor privacy portal. Verisk acquired Infutor Data Solutions. State data privacy notice covers CA, CO, CT, NJ, UT, VA and others.

steps.description: Go to Whitepages suppression request page
Enter the URL of your listing
Enter phone number for verification call
Receive automated verification call with code

optout.notes: Requires phone verification call. May need to remove from multiple listings.

meta.notes: Phone verification makes automation harder. CAPTCHA present.

scan.reason: B2B location data broker. No consumer-facing search.

steps.description: Go to X-Mode CCPA opt-out / Do Not Sell form
Enter your Device ID (Advertising ID or Installation ID)

optout.notes: FTC ordered X-Mode/Outlogic to stop selling sensitive location data (April 2024). Company sold data that could track visits to abortion clinics, places of worship, domestic violence shelters. Failed to honor Android opt-out requests. Requires Device ID (MAID or Installation ID) for opt-out. Sells data to government contractors and law enforcement.

meta.notes: Source: outlogic.io/privacy-policy, FTC enforcement action (Jan 2024, finalized Apr 2024). FTC prohibited from selling sensitive location data. Sold location data to hundreds of clients including government contractors and law enforcement. Collected data via own apps and third-party SDK partners. Rebranded from X-Mode Social to Outlogic.

steps.description: Scroll to bottom and click 'Suppress My Data' link
You will be redirected to PeopleConnect suppression center
Verify email via link from PeopleConnect (15 min expiry)
Select your record and set to Suppressed

optout.notes: Owned by Intelius/PeopleConnect. Redirects to PeopleConnect Suppression Center. Single suppression covers all PeopleConnect brands.

meta.notes: Subsidiary of Intelius/PeopleConnect. Suppression propagates across all PeopleConnect sites. Source: Big Ass Data Broker Opt-Out List, IntelTechniques Workbook

scan.reason: B2B sales intelligence platform. Requires paid subscription to search.

steps.description: Go to ZoomInfo Privacy Center removal page
Click 'Request Removal' to receive verification code
Check email for 6-digit verification code from ZoomInfo

optout.notes: Requires email verification with 6-digit code. Full name is optional on removal form. Profile removed from website and subscription products. Opt-out shared with ZoomInfo customers. Heavily scrapes LinkedIn.

meta.notes: Source: privacyrequest.zoominfo.com, zoominfo.com/trust-center/your-privacy, California Data Broker Registry. Largest B2B contact database. Scrapes LinkedIn, company websites, SEC filings, job boards. Claims GDPR and CCPA compliance.
